title: RENTERS' RIGHTS HOTLINE - 211info
text: COMMUNITY ALLIANCE OF TENANTS RENTERS' RIGHTS HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a renters' rights hotline in Oregon. Topics include fair housing protections, evictions, terminations, repair concerns, security deposits, and rent increase limits, etc. Does not provide information for landlords. Priority is given to renters in Southern and Central Oregon, Mid-Valley, and Portland/Tri-County area. Residents of all other Oregon regions strongly encouraged to become members to receive prioritized assistance. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Hotline intake and callbacks: Monday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday 1pm-5pm, Tuesday 5pm-8pm Member's Live Hotline: Tuesday 6pm-7pm, Saturday 1pm-2pm Email (503) 288-0130 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 288-0130 Renters' Rights Hotline Hotline (503) 288-0130 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for information. Provide name, number, city, Community Alliance of Tenants (CAT) membership status, brief description of issues, and best time to be reached within hotline business hours. Due to high call volume and funding cuts, response times may be delayed. FEES: Membership not required for residents in Southern and Central Oregon, Mid-Valley, and Portland/Tri-County area (Coos, Deschutes, Jackson, Marion, Multnomah, and Washington Counties). Residents of all other Oregon regions strongly encouraged to become members to receive prioritized assistance. Membership dues are $10 for annual membership or Working Memberships require volunteering for CAT for 2 hours. DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, Amharic, Tigrinya, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Housing Advocacy Groups Mold Pollution Information Pest Information * Housing Issues Tenant Associations Tenant Rights Information/Counseling If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
